race.name: Avilus
entries: Optimistic songbirds that are just as likable as they are fast.

entries.name: Age
entries: Most avilus live to about 150–200 years, aging only once they have about a decade left to live.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: All avilus are good without a doubt, but while most are lawful, a few adventurers lean chaotic.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Small.

entries.name: Avian Anatomy
entries: Your wings act as your hands for wielding weapons and casting spells. While flying or hovering, you can only take the Attack action with undersized weapons, using your toes to hold or draw them.

entries.name: Overwhelming Positivity
entries: You are proficient in the {@skill Persuasion} skill. You may double your proficiency bonus when making a Persuasion check on a creature currently in a negative emotional state (such as depression or anger).

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Your creature type is beast.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Auran.

Avilus are small, intelligent birds created long ago by a lonely wizard who accidentally granted a bird sentience. Since then, they have spread across the Material Plane and even into the Elemental Plane of Air.
They hide their intellect from most other races, but are known for their radiant positivity and their tendency to cheer up those who are sad or angry.

entries.name: Society
entries: Avilus live among other races, often acting as companions or counselors. They keep their ability to speak secret, but sometimes reveal it to comfort others.

entries.name: Names
entries: Avilus adopt the first names of other races, such as human, elven, or dwarven names.

entries.name: Male Names
entries: Aseir, Bardeid, Adran, Quarion, Taklinn, Timu, Thorin

entries.name: Female Names
entries: Atala, Ceidil, Jelenneth, Thia, Kathra, Mardred

race.name: Awoken Bread Loaf
entries: Awoken Loafs are animated bread loaves brought to life by wandering bakers’ magic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Awoken Loafs mature in just 2 weeks and live up to 50 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most Loafs lean toward chaotic alignments, though some find order in their lives.

entries.name: Size
entries: All Loafs are Tiny.

entries.name: Stale Crust
entries: If unarmored, your AC equals 14 + your Constitution modifier.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language based on your subrace.

entries.name: Open Your Third Rye
entries: You know the {@spell mage hand} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Stepchild of Lembas
entries: You gain the Wild Shape feature as if you were a 2nd-level Circle of the Moon druid, but you only have one use of Wild Shape.

entries.name: Ripe for the Pickings
entries: You know the {@spell prestidigitation} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Elelemenental
entries: Choose one spell based on your elemental plane: {@spell zephyr strike}, {@spell earth tremor}, {@spell armor of agathys}, or {@spell burning hands}. You can cast it once per short or long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Carbs Aren’t Bad
entries: You know the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Drake ’n’ Bake
entries: You gain a breath weapon identical to the Dragonborn’s breath weapon (PHB p.34).

entries.name: All Brain Loaf
entries: You know the {@spell mind sliver} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Wholy Wheat
entries: You gain resistance to radiant and necrotic damage.

entries.name: Unholy Darkvision
entries: You have darkvision out to 120 feet. Unlike normal darkvision, you see in full color.

entries.name: Uncrustable
entries: You may use your Constitution modifier in place of Strength or Dexterity for weapon attack and damage rolls.

Awoken Bread Loafs are whimsical, animated loaves of bread that spring to life from ovens touched by stray magic. They often wander into adventuring parties with no clear reason, bringing both comedy and unexpected resilience.

entries.name: Naming Conventions
entries: Loafs are named with a first name, followed by their type of loaf, a nickname as a middle name, and finally the surname of their baker.

race.name: Colossus
entries: Living castles created to defend and destroy, who unexpectedly have sentience and a soul.

entries.name: Age
entries: Colossi are created with adult-like intelligence and do not age in the conventional sense. After a few centuries their bodies crumble and can no longer hold together.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Though colossi have free will, they were created as dutiful soldiers. They lean strongly toward lawful alignments, with a smaller leaning toward good.

entries.name: Size
entries: Colossi are massive, often well over 8 feet tall. Your size is Large. If your campaign allows Huge player characters, you may choose to be Huge instead. You weigh thousands of pounds.

entries.name: Type
entries: Your creature types are humanoid and construct. Effects that target either type affect you.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet. You cannot fly by any means, but can be carried by flying vehicles. You cannot swim, but instead walk along the bottom of water at half speed.

entries.name: Stone Skin
entries: You can add your Constitution modifier to your AC instead of Dexterity. You cannot do so with an effect that already adds Constitution to AC, such as a barbarian’s Unarmored Defense.

entries.name: Partial Construct Nature
entries: You cannot eat, drink, or breathe, and cannot benefit from potions. You must still sleep, but can complete a long rest in half the usual time.

entries.name: Siege Sentinel
entries: Objects of Large size or greater (including vehicles without an Intelligence score) are vulnerable to damage you deal.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common.

entries.name: Arm Cannon
entries: One of your arms is a cannon (if firearms exist) or a ballista (if not). You are proficient with it. Range 150/600 ft. Attack and damage use Strength or Dexterity (your choice). Damage: 1d8 bludgeoning (cannon) or piercing (ballista), scaling to 1d10 at 5th level and 1d12 at 11th. If you Ready an attack against a flying or Gargantuan target, the attack roll has advantage.

entries.name: Vantage Angle
entries: Your speed isn’t reduced by climbing, and you are acclimated to high altitudes.

entries.name: Magic Deflection
entries: You always have at least half cover against ranged spell attack rolls.

entries.name: Ultimate Defense
entries: When you take the Dodge action on a turn in which you neither move nor take any other actions (including bonus actions), your half cover extends to all attacks and effects until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Ramming Speed
entries: Once per turn, if you move at least 30 feet in a straight line, you can immediately move an additional 15 feet in the same direction without expending movement.

entries.name: Unflinching
entries: If a Medium or smaller creature would knock you prone or shove you 5 feet or less, you can use your reaction to prevent it.

Colossi are living castles, massive constructs of stone or brick given life to defend or destroy entire cities. Though created as weapons of war, they have gained free will and are now recognized as living beings. Many still struggle with their origins as soldiers, but they form strong bonds with others of their kind and with those who respect their plight.

race.name: Cyborg
entries: Not all people are delighted with their humanity. Some may think it to be a harness, rather than a blessing. There are those who seek to always break their limits, get stronger, faster, better. Then, there are the Cyborgs, those who improve on their bodies by stripping away that which made them human in the first place. Often times, for these half-humanoids, adventure is the best way to achieve further improvement, further growth and to truly knock down the boundaries of a human life they find too plain for their liking.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Intelligence score increases by 1. Based on your subrace, another ability score increases by 2. Alternatively, when determining your character's ability scores, increase any one ability score by 2 and a different ability score by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: A cyborg's age is defined by the race they were before heavily altering their body. If they were an elf, they are likely to live for much longer than if they were a human. However, with enough experimentation, a cyborg might make their body entirely ageless.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Due to their inclination for altering and removing traditionally humanoid traits, cyborgs are often very neutral, but their experimentation also requires precision and lawfulness.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small. You choose your size when you gain this race.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 35 feet.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid and a Construct.

entries.name: Mechanical Endurance
entries: You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Cybernetic Knowledge
entries: You have proficiency in {@item tinker's tools|phb} and the {@skill Arcana} skill.

entries.name: Mechano-organic Anatomy
entries: Due to your partially humanoid anatomy, your consumption of resources is reduced. You only need to consume food and water once every 3 days. In order to gain the benefits of a long rest, you only need to remain seated for 6 hours, during which you are partially awake and aware of your surroundings.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice, which can reflect your original race or background.

subraces.name: Gearhead
entries: Gearheads make alterations mainly to their skulls, adding cogs, cybernetic eyes, and other modifications that allow them to better use their mental prowess.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Due to your cybernetic eyes, you can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

subraces.name: Clockheart
entries: Whether it be because of a weakened immune system or because of the drive to push the body further than its natural limits, clockhearts swap out internal organs for cybernetic implants, thus becoming bastions of inner strength.

entries.name: Cybernetic Immuno-protectors
entries: You have advantage on all saving throws against disease, exhaustion, and on death saving throws.

entries.name: Accelerated Mending
entries: When you roll Hit Dice to restore hit points, you may roll twice and choose the higher result.

entries.name: Clockheart Recalibrating
entries: Once per long rest, you may attempt to recalibrate your inner mechanisms in order to dispel harmful effects. If you are under a spell or magical effect that requires a saving throw, you may spend your bonus action to make that saving throw once again, ending it on a success.

subraces.name: Cyberstep
entries: Whether it be because of a weakened immune system or because of the drive to push the body further than its natural limits, clockhearts swap out internal organs for cybernetic implants, thus becoming bastions of inner strength.

entries.name: Enhanced Cyberjoints
entries: Because of your empowered legs, your movement speed is increased by 5 feet and your jump distance is tripled.

entries.name: Step Propulsor
entries: Once per long rest, you may activate your step propulsors. For the next minute, your walking speed is doubled.

Cyborgs replace flesh with artifice in pursuit of strength, precision, and transcendence. Many become adventurers because danger, discovery, and treasure offer the fastest path to further augmentation.

entries.name: Society
entries: Cyborgs rarely form societies of their own. Instead, they emerge from other cultures and either remain within them as altered outliers or leave to pursue ever greater self-improvement.

entries.name: Names
entries: Cyborgs often keep the names of the people they once were, though some adopt sharper, more mechanical monikers after extensive modification.

race.name: House Dragon
entries: Small, quadrupedal dragons who aren’t afraid to go out adventuring with their bipedal peers.

entries.name: Age
entries: House dragons reach maturity in about 10 years, and live to be about 50; not nearly as long as larger dragons, mainly due to the breeding process.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Alignment tendencies were bred out of your species long ago, so your alignment tends to lean toward whatever group of non‑House Dragons you grew up with.

entries.name: Size
entries: House dragons stand 2 to 3 feet at the shoulder and weigh around 100 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Feral Stance
entries: You are a quadruped. Your forepaws have opposable thumbs, allowing you to manipulate objects, but you cannot properly wield weapons made for bipeds. You cannot use two‑handed weapons or dual‑wield, and you cannot don armor made for bipeds (you require barding). Shields do not grant AC bonuses. Actions requiring free hands (loading weapons, drinking potions, casting spells with material components) cost 5 feet of movement.

entries.name: Natural Defenses
entries: Your scales provide protection. You gain a +1 bonus to AC.

entries.name: Natural Offenses
entries: You have a claw attack and a bite attack, considered unarmed strikes. They deal 1d6 + Strength modifier damage. Claws deal slashing damage and have the finesse property; bites deal piercing damage.

entries.name: Big Eater
entries: You have advantage on checks to resist poison and disease transmitted via food and drink, and on checks to consume large or questionable amounts of food and drink.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

House dragons are miniature, wingless dragons bred to live indoors. They lack breath weapons and wings, but retain draconic charisma and resilience. They serve as companions, familiars, and even adventurers.

entries.name: Society
entries: House dragons live among other species rather than forming their own societies. They are social and adapt to the culture they are raised in.

entries.name: Names
entries: House dragons adopt the naming conventions of the cultures they live with, rather than having their own.

entries.name: Random Height and Weight
entries: Base Height: 2'0" + 1d12 in.
Base Weight: 100 lb. × (1d12)

variants.name: Coward Mode
entries: Variant rules for players who don’t want to deal with quadruped restrictions:
- You may apply proficiency to a single one‑handed weapon wielded in your mouth.
- You may fulfill somatic components by wagging your tail, and material components by merely having them on your person.
- Movement penalties from Feral Stance no longer apply.

race.name: Kemonomimi
entries: Kemonomimi are humanoids with animal-like features such as ears, tails, wings, or horns. Their appearance varies widely depending on their subrace.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

subraces.name: Bear-Eared
entries: You have the small round bear ears and a stubby bear tail.

entries.name: Hibernation Preparation
entries: Bears need to hibernate through the winter, but you don't. After eating at least 1 pound of food beyond your requirement for the day, you gain 1d6 temporary hit points for the next hour.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Bears have a good sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Mama Bear
entries: When an ally creature is hit while you're within 5 feet of them, you can use your reaction to do a melee weapon attack on the attacker if they are within your weapon's reach. You regain usage of this feature when you finish a short or long rest.

subraces.name: Cow-Eared
entries: You have floppy cow ears, a long furry cow tail and the horns of a cow/bull.

entries.name: Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line towards a creature and then make a successful attack with your horns, the target must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. The DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Horns
entries: In addition to your cow-ears, you have natural horns. When you do an unarmed attack, you can instead use your horns to do 1d6 piercing damage using your Strength as attack and damage modifiers as normal.

entries.name: Improved Digestion
entries: Your single stomach has more digestive compartments, and thus you can eat things humans normally can't digest like grass and it'd count as food for you. However, you need twice the amount of food a normal Medium-sized creature requires each day.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

subraces.name: Wolf-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a wolf and fluffy wolf's tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Smell and Hearing
entries: Wolves have a good sense of smell and hearing, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pack Tactics
entries: You have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the your allies is within 5 ft. of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

subraces.name: Lion-Eared
entries: You have the Fluffy ears of a Lion and fluffy Lion's tail.

entries.name: Teeth and Claws
entries: You have retractable claws and very sharp teeth, this allows you to do an unarmed attack of 1D8 + Str with your Bite or 1D6 + Str with your claws.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Lions have a good sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pack Tactics
entries: You have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the your allies is within 5 ft. of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

subraces.name: Cat-Eared
entries: You have the triangle-shaped ears of a cat and a long furry cat tail.

entries.name: Cat Burglar
entries: As a cat, you enter and exit without being noticed. You are proficient in Stealth.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Hunter
entries: You are a natural hunter, and you focus on your prey with conviction. You can cast the hunter's mark spell once. You regain usage of this feature after you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Naturally Graceful
entries: Cats always land on their feet. You have resistance to fall damage.

subraces.name: Feather-Eared
entries: You have small feathered wings where your ears are, a feathered bird tail, along with their feathered wings.

entries.name: Bird Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have bird wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground. You may not fly in normal backpacks or armor. The cost to alter your gear to be suitable for flight is equal to half the cost of the gear.

entries.name: Keen Sight
entries: Birds are very good at spotting and finding prey. You are proficient in Perception.

subraces.name: Raccoon-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a raccoon and a stripped furry fail

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Natural Bandit
entries: As a scavenger, raccoons naturally steal things. You are proficient in Sleight of Hand and Thieves' Tools.

entries.name: Tree Climber
entries: Raccoons can climb trees, but since you have human-like hands, you can't climb as well. When attempting to climb trees with an Athletics check, you may use Dexterity as the modifier instead of Strength.

subraces.name: Horse-Eared
entries: You have the ears of a horse or pony, along with their long-haired tail.

entries.name: Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then attempt to shove it to knock it prone, the target instead must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. The DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Swift Steed
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Work Horse
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can’t use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Pig-Eared
entries: You have the large ears of a pig and their coiled tails

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Pigs are very good at foraging with their nose, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pig Fat
entries: Your fat gives you more endurance, your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

subraces.name: Sheep-Eared
entries: You have the long pointed ears of a sheep, along with their wool-like hair, curved horns, and cute little tail.

entries.name: Horns
entries: In addition to your sheep-ears, you have natural curved horns. Your unarmed strikes using your Horns deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Sure-Footed
entries: You have advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock you prone.

entries.name: Woolly Hair
entries: Instead of hair, you grow wool on your body instead, giving you extra protection. When unarmored, your Armor Class is equal to 12 + your Dexterity modifier.

subraces.name: Bat-Eared
entries: You have the large wide ears of a bat, bat wings growing out your back, and possibly a tail if your species had one.

entries.name: Bat Roost
entries: When grabbing fixtures on a ceiling (such as chandeliers or stalactites), or long appendages sticking out (such as a flagpole sticking on the side or branches of trees), you can hang upside-down on them without making an ability check if you occupy both your hands on it. You cannot hang upside down on smooth surfaces where there's nothing to grab onto. However, you're not a real bat and you have human hands instead of claws, so the blood will rush to your head and you will lose your grip. Thus, you can only hang like this for a minute without moving. At the end of the minute, if you did not move, you start falling.

entries.name: Bat Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have bat wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. But due to the short wingspan due to being a kemonomimi, while flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any medium or heavy armor, you may not fly.

entries.name: Echolocation
entries: You can perceive your surroundings without relying on sight within a specific radius. You have a blindsight range of 10 feet. You can't use your blindsight while deafened.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Chromatic)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Chromatic Protection
entries: Starting at 3rd level, as an action, you can channel your draconic energies to protect yourself. For 10 minutes, you become resistant to the damage type associated with your Chromatic Ancestry. Once you use this trait, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Metallic)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Metallic Breath
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second breath weapon. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhale in a 15-foot cone. The saving throw DC for this breath is 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. Whenever you use this feature, choose one: Unnerving Breath. Each creature in the cone must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the start of your next turn. Repulsion Breath.* Each creature in the cone must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 20 feet away from you and fall to the ground.Once you use your Metal Breath, you can't do it again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Gem)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Gem Rarity
entries: Unlike many Dragonborns, you gain a Draconic breath that is different from the others. Choose one from Psychic, Radiant, Strength, Necrotic, or Thunder.

entries.name: Psionic Mind
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You don't have to share a language with the creature, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. You have the creature talk to you for up to 1 hour. To do this, she needs to see and be aware to do this or you will terminate this effect (no action needed).

entries.name: Gift of the Gem Dragons
entries: You know the Silver Mind trick, which you use at will. Starting at 3rd level, you gain Tasha's steric laughter spell. Starting at 5th level, you gain the Detect Thoughts spell. Once you use one of the high-level spells, you can't use it again after a short or long rest. Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma is your ability to cast these spells.

subraces.name: Mouse-Eared
entries: You have the large round ears of a mouse and their long pointed tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Mice have an amazing sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Nimble
entries: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

entries.name: Scurry
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

subraces.name: Monkey-Eared
entries: You look almost entirely human, except for your long furry monkey tail.

entries.name: Prehensile Tail
entries: Your monkey tail can be used to hold and manipulate objects. However, your tail is not as strong as your arms, and thus, you cannot use your tail to hold things heavier than 10 pounds, nor use it to wield weapons with the heavy or two-handed property.

entries.name: Smart Monkey
entries: You can speak, read and write one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Tree Climber
entries: Monkeys climb and swing from trees, you are proficient in Acrobatics.

subraces.name: Rabbit-Eared
entries: You have a pair of floppy or pointed rabbit ears in addition to a small fluff ball tail.

entries.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 40 feet.

entries.name: Lucky Rabbit's Foot
entries: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

entries.name: Superior Hearing
entries: Your large rabbit ears grants you an increased awareness of your surroundings compared to other kemonomimi. You are proficient in Perception.

subraces.name: Squirrel-Eared
entries: You have pointed squirrel ears in addition to your large fluffy curved tail.

entries.name: Constant Prey
entries: People are always out for your big fluffy tail, you have a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) score.

entries.name: Forager
entries: Squirrels can naturally find food. You are proficient in Survival and you have advantage on Survival checks when foraging.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: Squirrels are very good at noticing threats, you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

subraces.name: Dog-Eared
entries: You have a variety of dog ears to pick from in addition to their wide variety of different tails, appearing like the breed you are.

entries.name: Alpha Dog
entries: You are inspiring and a natural leader. You know the guidance cantrip. Additionally, you can cast heroism using Charisma as your spellcasting ability modifier. You regain the use of this feature when you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: Dogs have good hearing and a sense of smell, you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Puppy Dog Eyes
entries: No one can withstand your puppy dog eyes. You gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

subraces.name: Fin-Eared
entries: You have the pectoral fins of the fish you are where your ears are, but no tail, however your seemingly human pair of legs transform and appear as your tail fin allowing you to move very fast in the water.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: It is very dark underwater where you live. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fins
entries: Your legs are your tail. As a bonus action, you can combine your legs for form your fish tail. Anything you're wearing from the waist down is automatically removed and is dropped on the floor unless it can fit your legs' new form. While in this form, your base walking speed is reduced to 15 feet and you gain a base swim speed of 50 feet.

entries.name: Gills
entries: You are the kemonomimi of the sea, and thus you can breathe air and water. You are immune to drowning.

subraces.name: Fox-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a fox and their bushy tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fox Cunning
entries: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

entries.name: Foxy
entries: Your foxy demeanor makes you hard to resist, before making a Charisma based skill check you can choose to grant yourself advantage. You regain usage of this feature when you complete a long rest.

subraces.name: Tiger-Eared
entries: You have small ears and a large tiger tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Born Survivor
entries: Choose 2 skills from: Athletics, Perception, Survival, Insight, Investigation and Nature to become proficient in.

entries.name: Sharp nails
entries: Your nails are like a usable natural weapon. You deal 1d6 slashing damage instead of bludgeoning.

entries.name: Ferocious Roar
entries: You enter a state of rage that reveals itself in a scream of animalistic fury. All creatures within 60 feet of you must make a Wisdom Saving Throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + Your Strength score) ​​or become frightened of you for 1 minute. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

race.name: Kumiho
entries: You are a 9 tailed foxes that like to take the form of humanoids, you gain the following traits:

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2.

entries.name: Age
entries: Kumiho reach adulthood around 150 years and can live up to a millenia.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most Kumiho tend to be chaotic, but on occasion they can be lawful to those they are fond of. Kumiho have been known to be both good or evil.

entries.name: Size
entries: 4 and half to 5 and a half feet tall in human or hybrid form and the size of a large dog in fox form. Your size is Medium in human and hybrid form and Small in fox form.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet. your movement is increased to 40 feet in fox form.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Incarnation
entries: You are able to change form to that of a human with your ears and tail(s) still visible or to a human-fox hybrid form. While in normal fox form, people cannot see you as anything more than a normal fox, unless they succeed a Perception check with a DC of 10 + your level. Switching forms requires an action. When in fox form, the only spells you can cast are cantrips.

entries.name: Tooth and Claw
entries: Kumiho have natural weapons, their claws do 1d4 + your Strength or your Dexterity modifier. When in fox form, you also have a bite attack that does 1d6 + your Strength or your Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Kumiho Tails
entries: You gain more tails as you gain more power starting with a second tail at level 4 you gain 1 tail every 2 levels up to 9 tails at level 18th.

entries.name: Acute Senses
entries: Kumiho have extraordinary senses. You have advantage on Perception checks.

entries.name: Sneaky
entries: You are proficient with the Stealth skill, and have advantage on Stealth checks but only while in fox form.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Fey, Descendent, Half-breed, Draconic.

entries.name: Physical Description
entries: At first glance, a kumiho is identical to a fox in every way, with the exception of long slender legs and angled bodies that rival the size of a large dog and most have 9 tails by the time they reach full adulthood. Their fur can come in different colors, but red with white tipped ears and tail is the most common coloration to be found aside from black tipped tail and ears or pure white fur. Their eyes illuminate with vibrant colors, making their iris have a faint glow in darkness.

Many kumiho like to travel in a humanoid form that displays their claws, ears, and tail(s). When a kumiho takes the form of a humanoid, they take the appearance of how their subconscious believes they would look as a human, but they always take the same appearance. They can also take a hybrid form, blending the appearance of their human and fox forms into an anthropomorphic fox.

Kumiho look very young for their actual age. One that is 100 years of age might look like a teenager. Around 150 years, their appearance halts around a human’s mid 20's until they reach 800 years. In the course of those final two centuries, they begin to age into late adulthood and finally their senior years prior to dying of old age.

entries.name: History
entries: Kumiho have roots among the fey as a race of mystical foxes incarnated from the souls of children who die at a young age. They often remain in the feywilds of their birth but the rare kumiho will venture out to see the worlds, hiding their true nature from curious eyes as they use fey magic to shift into a human form.

entries.name: Society
entries: Kumiho are widely believed to be a myth or thought to be the ancestors of Kitsune. Many scholars believe that Kumiho blood has powerful magic properties worth exploiting. When discovered, Kumiho will often claim they are a typical kitsune with a genetic mutation to have multiple tails. As Kumiho are often attractive and easy on the eyes, they are typically well liked in social interactions, except their hybrid form is usually unsettling to others.

entries.name: Male
entries: Mako, Kobe, Kurt, Kurama

entries.name: Female
entries: Holo, Myra, Lira, Asuna

subrace.name: Fey
entries: Your connection to the earliest of kumiho is strong, and your fey-like presence reflects this.

entries.name: Fey Magic
entries: You know the produce flame cantrip, with the exception that your flame is blue. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the charm person spell once and need to finish a long rest before you can cast it again. When you reach 5th level, you can also cast the phantasmal force spell once and need to finish a long rest before you can cast it again. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Sylvan Aura
entries: You give off an aura of fey magic constantly. Creatures that can see or detect magic see a miasma of magic surrounding you in a 5 foot radius. This aura alerts you to unseen threats within 5 feet. This acts as blindsense up to 5 feet, only telling you a direction, not position or identity of the creature or object.

subrace.name: Descendent
entries: Your parents have strayed from the feywild forests and have adapted to surviving with their fox form.

entries.name: Wild Survivor
entries: You were raised in the wild and know how to survive in the woods. You have advantage on Nature and Survival checks.

entries.name: Natural Hunter
entries: Your ability to hunt has taught you how to stalk and pounce. You have advantage with Stealth checks in any form. Also, you may use your Dexterity score when determining your jumping distance and add your Dexterity modifier to any Athletics checks associated with jumping.

subrace.name: Half-breed
entries: With such beautiful human forms, it was only a matter of time for a half-breed kumiho to be born.

entries.name: Half Blood
entries: In your human form your claws, tail, and ears are not visible if you wish to hide them. Creatures can identify you as a half-blood kumiho using an Investigation check with a DC of 12 + your level. Your "human" form takes the appearance of your half-blood parentage instead of human but you are still Medium sized.

entries.name: Family Connection
entries: Select a race other than kumiho to be the base of your other parent. You may select a subrace from that race and benefit from it's traits. If the chosen race does't have a subrace (e.g. Human) you may choose one trait from the race. The chosen trait can't be ability score increase, alignment, speed, size, or age.

subrace.name: Draconic
entries: Somewhere in your lineage, dragon blood or a dragon’s blessing found its way to you. Unlike other kumiho, you have a pair of dragon-like horns and eyes, and some parts of your dragon body may even have patches of scales. The horns and eyes are visible in your human form. Pick a dragon color from the table below during character creation to determine your subrace trait elements. **Discuss this subrace with you DM before selecting it.

entries.name: Ancestral Breath
entries: Your bloodline has blessed you with a breath attack. As a action, you may use a breath attack matching the element and shape of the dragon color you have selected from the table below. The damage increases to 2d6 at 6th level, 3d6 at 11th level, and 4d6 at 16th level. The target creature may make the appropriate save with a DC of 12 + Constitution modifier. On a successful save they take half damage. You may use this trait once and regain its use after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Form
entries: You can take the form of a dragon once. This form is a human sized chromatic or metallic dragon of the color you selected. You are medium while in this form, have a fly speed of 30ft, and have advantage to Constitution saves. You can freely use your ancestral breath while in this form a number of times equal to your level, but lose your ancestral breath. This transformation lasts 1 minute and you regain its use after finishing a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Type
entries: You can select one of the following Dragon Types to be your dragon form.

items.name: Black
entries: Type: Acid - Breath Weapon: 5 by 30 ft line (Dex Save)

items.name: Blue
entries: Type: Lightning - Breath Weapon: 5 by 30 ft line (Dex Save)

items.name: Brass
entries: Type: Fire - Breath Weapon: 5 by 30 ft line (Dex Save)

items.name: Bronze
entries: Type: Lightning - Breath Weapon: 5 by 30 ft line (Dex Save)

items.name: Copper
entries: Type: Acid - Breath Weapon: 5 by 30 ft line (Dex Save)

items.name: Gold
entries: Type: Fire - Breath Weapon: 15 ft cone (Dex Save)

items.name: Green
entries: Type: Poison - Breath Weapon: 15 ft cone (Con Save)

items.name: Red
entries: Type: Fire - Breath Weapon: 15 ft cone (Dex Save)

items.name: Silver
entries: Type: Cold - Breath Weapon: 15 ft cone (Con Save)

items.name: White
entries: Type: Cold - Breath Weapon: 15 ft cone (Con Save)

race.name: Lopunny
entries: Graceful, beautiful, and rather short, these naturally timid bipedal lagomorphs resemble rabbits with long ears and fluffy fur.

entries.name: Age
entries: Bunearies reach maturity and develop into Lopunnies at 2 years and can live for approximately 12 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Lopunnies usually lean toward neutral or lawful alignments, though mostly neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: The average height for a Lopunny is barely 4 feet tall and usually weighs a little over 70 lbs. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Timid Nature
entries: You have disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened.

entries.name: Burst of Speed
entries: When you take the Dash action, you can move an additional 10 feet.

entries.name: Leg Up
entries: You have proficiency with using your legs to perform unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

entries.name: Cute Charm
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Limber
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

entries.name: Heightened Hearing
entries: Whenever you make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check that involves hearing, you are considered proficient in the {@skill Perception} skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and your choice of Elvish or Sylvan.
Graceful and cautious, Lopunnies are timid lagomorphs who prefer to flee danger but can deliver powerful kicks when forced to fight.

entries.name: History
entries: Lopunnies, along with their Buneary young, originated from the forests of the Sinnoh region, though they can be found almost anywhere. They are favorites among coordinators and contest participants due to their charm.

entries.name: Society
entries: Lopunnies usually live in colonies, caring for their young. They shed their fur twice a year, which is often collected and used in clothing.

entries.name: Names
entries: Despite appearing feminine, Lopunnies can be either male or female. Their names are usually Common or Elvish.

entries.name: Random Height and Weight
entries: Base Height: 3'6" + 2d4 in.
Base Weight: 60 lb. × (1d4)

race.name: Pipe Fox
entries: Curious fox spirits with serpentine bodies, pipe foxes are subtle collectors of knowledge who trade secrets as readily as coin.

entries.name: Age
entries: Pipe foxes mature by age 8 and can live for around 120 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Pipe foxes are usually neutral, balancing playful curiosity with careful self-preservation.

entries.name: Size
entries: Pipe foxes are small, lithe creatures with long bodies and foxlike heads. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a climbing speed of 20 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Lorekeeper
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill.

entries.name: Natural Bite
entries: You have a bite attack that you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, the strike deals 1d4 piercing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Fox Magic
entries: You know the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast {@spell invisibility} with this trait once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

A pipe fox is a furry, serpentine fox spirit that values knowledge above nearly everything else. Pipe foxes hoard secrets, lore, and rumors, sharing them only when they are offered something equally valuable in return.

entries.name: Society
entries: Pipe foxes rarely gather in large communities. Most instead attach themselves to scholars, mystics, or travelers whose lives promise a steady flow of stories and hidden truths.

entries.name: Names
entries: Pipe fox names tend to be short, musical, and easy to whisper: Kiko, Nari, Suzu, Tama, Yori, and Zeni are common examples.

race.name: Robot
entries: Artificial beings of metal and circuitry, robots are manufactured constructs that have gained sentience.

entries.name: Age
entries: Robots do not age naturally. They can function indefinitely with proper maintenance, though most break down after a few centuries.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Robots tend toward lawful alignments, following their programming, though free‑willed robots may adopt any alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Robots are built in humanoid form, typically between 5 and 7 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Constructed Nature
entries: You are a mass of metals, wires, and whatnot. Your creature type is construct instead of humanoid. As such, spells like cure wounds don't affect you, and you are immune to spells like crown of madness or dominate person because they specifically target humanoids. You are immune to poison damage, being poisoned, and diseases. You do not need to eat or breathe, and you cannot attempt to do so. Rather than sleeping, you enter an inactive state for 6 hours each day. You do not dream in this state; you are fully aware of your surroundings and notice approaching enemies and other events as normal.

entries.name: Integrated Protection
entries: Your AC can never be lower than 10 + your Constitution modifier.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common.

entries.name: War Proficiency
entries: You are proficient with any four martial weapons of your choice.

entries.name: Fast
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Battle Dash
entries: You can move up to half your walking speed after taking an attack action. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses when you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Manufacturer Robot
languages: one other language of your choice

entries.name: Mercantile Programming
entries: You learn one language of your choice.

entries.name: Manufacture
entries: You are proficient in an artisan's tool of your choice. Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a single simple item worth no more than 10 gp. The item should be relevant to the nature of the tool you are proficient in. For example, leatherworker's tools would allow you to make a simple belt or a cloak. Glassblower's tools might allow you to make a simple, but crude vase.

entries.name: Quality Check
entries: Whenever you make an ({@skill Investigation}) check related to defects in a product, you are considered proficient in the Investigation skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Medical Sensor
entries: As an action, you can sense if any creatures within 60 feet of you are currently affected by poisons or diseases. You do not know what the poison or disease is.

entries.name: First Aid
entries: You can cast cure wounds a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, using Wisdom as your spellcasting modifier. You regain use of this trait after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Medical Knowledge
entries: You are proficient in Medicine.
Robots are manufactured constructs, often created for war, labor, or specialized tasks. Over time, some have developed free will and independence, becoming adventurers or companions.

entries.name: Society
entries: Robots do not form societies of their own, instead integrating into the cultures of their creators or wandering as individuals.

entries.name: Names
entries: Robots are often designated by serial numbers or functional titles, though some adopt personal names.

race.name: Avilus
entries: Optimistic songbirds that are just as likable as they are fast.

entries.name: Age
entries: Most avilus live to about 150–200 years, aging only once they have about a decade left to live.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: All avilus are good without a doubt, but while most are lawful, a few adventurers lean chaotic.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Small.

entries.name: Avian Anatomy
entries: Your wings act as your hands for wielding weapons and casting spells. While flying or hovering, you can only take the Attack action with undersized weapons, using your toes to hold or draw them.

entries.name: Overwhelming Positivity
entries: You are proficient in the {@skill Persuasion} skill. You may double your proficiency bonus when making a Persuasion check on a creature currently in a negative emotional state (such as depression or anger).

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Your creature type is beast.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Auran.

Avilus are small, intelligent birds created long ago by a lonely wizard who accidentally granted a bird sentience. Since then, they have spread across the Material Plane and even into the Elemental Plane of Air.
They hide their intellect from most other races, but are known for their radiant positivity and their tendency to cheer up those who are sad or angry.

entries.name: Society
entries: Avilus live among other races, often acting as companions or counselors. They keep their ability to speak secret, but sometimes reveal it to comfort others.

entries.name: Names
entries: Avilus adopt the first names of other races, such as human, elven, or dwarven names.

entries.name: Male Names
entries: Aseir, Bardeid, Adran, Quarion, Taklinn, Timu, Thorin

entries.name: Female Names
entries: Atala, Ceidil, Jelenneth, Thia, Kathra, Mardred

race.name: Awoken Bread Loaf
entries: Awoken Loafs are animated bread loaves brought to life by wandering bakers’ magic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Awoken Loafs mature in just 2 weeks and live up to 50 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most Loafs lean toward chaotic alignments, though some find order in their lives.

entries.name: Size
entries: All Loafs are Tiny.

entries.name: Stale Crust
entries: If unarmored, your AC equals 14 + your Constitution modifier.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language based on your subrace.

entries.name: Open Your Third Rye
entries: You know the {@spell mage hand} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Stepchild of Lembas
entries: You gain the Wild Shape feature as if you were a 2nd-level Circle of the Moon druid, but you only have one use of Wild Shape.

entries.name: Ripe for the Pickings
entries: You know the {@spell prestidigitation} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Elelemenental
entries: Choose one spell based on your elemental plane: {@spell zephyr strike}, {@spell earth tremor}, {@spell armor of agathys}, or {@spell burning hands}. You can cast it once per short or long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Carbs Aren’t Bad
entries: You know the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Drake ’n’ Bake
entries: You gain a breath weapon identical to the Dragonborn’s breath weapon (PHB p.34).

entries.name: All Brain Loaf
entries: You know the {@spell mind sliver} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Wholy Wheat
entries: You gain resistance to radiant and necrotic damage.

entries.name: Unholy Darkvision
entries: You have darkvision out to 120 feet. Unlike normal darkvision, you see in full color.

entries.name: Uncrustable
entries: You may use your Constitution modifier in place of Strength or Dexterity for weapon attack and damage rolls.

Awoken Bread Loafs are whimsical, animated loaves of bread that spring to life from ovens touched by stray magic. They often wander into adventuring parties with no clear reason, bringing both comedy and unexpected resilience.

entries.name: Naming Conventions
entries: Loafs are named with a first name, followed by their type of loaf, a nickname as a middle name, and finally the surname of their baker.

race.name: Colossus
entries: Living castles created to defend and destroy, who unexpectedly have sentience and a soul.

entries.name: Age
entries: Colossi are created with adult-like intelligence and do not age in the conventional sense. After a few centuries their bodies crumble and can no longer hold together.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Though colossi have free will, they were created as dutiful soldiers. They lean strongly toward lawful alignments, with a smaller leaning toward good.

entries.name: Size
entries: Colossi are massive, often well over 8 feet tall. Your size is Large. If your campaign allows Huge player characters, you may choose to be Huge instead. You weigh thousands of pounds.

entries.name: Type
entries: Your creature types are humanoid and construct. Effects that target either type affect you.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet. You cannot fly by any means, but can be carried by flying vehicles. You cannot swim, but instead walk along the bottom of water at half speed.

entries.name: Stone Skin
entries: You can add your Constitution modifier to your AC instead of Dexterity. You cannot do so with an effect that already adds Constitution to AC, such as a barbarian’s Unarmored Defense.

entries.name: Partial Construct Nature
entries: You cannot eat, drink, or breathe, and cannot benefit from potions. You must still sleep, but can complete a long rest in half the usual time.

entries.name: Siege Sentinel
entries: Objects of Large size or greater (including vehicles without an Intelligence score) are vulnerable to damage you deal.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common.

entries.name: Arm Cannon
entries: One of your arms is a cannon (if firearms exist) or a ballista (if not). You are proficient with it. Range 150/600 ft. Attack and damage use Strength or Dexterity (your choice). Damage: 1d8 bludgeoning (cannon) or piercing (ballista), scaling to 1d10 at 5th level and 1d12 at 11th. If you Ready an attack against a flying or Gargantuan target, the attack roll has advantage.

entries.name: Vantage Angle
entries: Your speed isn’t reduced by climbing, and you are acclimated to high altitudes.

entries.name: Magic Deflection
entries: You always have at least half cover against ranged spell attack rolls.

entries.name: Ultimate Defense
entries: When you take the Dodge action on a turn in which you neither move nor take any other actions (including bonus actions), your half cover extends to all attacks and effects until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Ramming Speed
entries: Once per turn, if you move at least 30 feet in a straight line, you can immediately move an additional 15 feet in the same direction without expending movement.

entries.name: Unflinching
entries: If a Medium or smaller creature would knock you prone or shove you 5 feet or less, you can use your reaction to prevent it.

Colossi are living castles, massive constructs of stone or brick given life to defend or destroy entire cities. Though created as weapons of war, they have gained free will and are now recognized as living beings. Many still struggle with their origins as soldiers, but they form strong bonds with others of their kind and with those who respect their plight.

race.name: Cyborg
entries: Not all people are delighted with their humanity. Some may think it to be a harness, rather than a blessing. There are those who seek to always break their limits, get stronger, faster, better. Then, there are the Cyborgs, those who improve on their bodies by stripping away that which made them human in the first place. Often times, for these half-humanoids, adventure is the best way to achieve further improvement, further growth and to truly knock down the boundaries of a human life they find too plain for their liking.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Intelligence score increases by 1. Based on your subrace, another ability score increases by 2. Alternatively, when determining your character's ability scores, increase any one ability score by 2 and a different ability score by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: A cyborg's age is defined by the race they were before heavily altering their body. If they were an elf, they are likely to live for much longer than if they were a human. However, with enough experimentation, a cyborg might make their body entirely ageless.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Due to their inclination for altering and removing traditionally humanoid traits, cyborgs are often very neutral, but their experimentation also requires precision and lawfulness.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small. You choose your size when you gain this race.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 35 feet.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are a Humanoid and a Construct.

entries.name: Mechanical Endurance
entries: You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Cybernetic Knowledge
entries: You have proficiency in {@item tinker's tools|phb} and the {@skill Arcana} skill.

entries.name: Mechano-organic Anatomy
entries: Due to your partially humanoid anatomy, your consumption of resources is reduced. You only need to consume food and water once every 3 days. In order to gain the benefits of a long rest, you only need to remain seated for 6 hours, during which you are partially awake and aware of your surroundings.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice, which can reflect your original race or background.

subraces.name: Gearhead
entries: Gearheads make alterations mainly to their skulls, adding cogs, cybernetic eyes, and other modifications that allow them to better use their mental prowess.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Due to your cybernetic eyes, you can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

subraces.name: Clockheart
entries: Whether it be because of a weakened immune system or because of the drive to push the body further than its natural limits, clockhearts swap out internal organs for cybernetic implants, thus becoming bastions of inner strength.

entries.name: Cybernetic Immuno-protectors
entries: You have advantage on all saving throws against disease, exhaustion, and on death saving throws.

entries.name: Accelerated Mending
entries: When you roll Hit Dice to restore hit points, you may roll twice and choose the higher result.

entries.name: Clockheart Recalibrating
entries: Once per long rest, you may attempt to recalibrate your inner mechanisms in order to dispel harmful effects. If you are under a spell or magical effect that requires a saving throw, you may spend your bonus action to make that saving throw once again, ending it on a success.

subraces.name: Cyberstep
entries: Whether it be because of a weakened immune system or because of the drive to push the body further than its natural limits, clockhearts swap out internal organs for cybernetic implants, thus becoming bastions of inner strength.

entries.name: Enhanced Cyberjoints
entries: Because of your empowered legs, your movement speed is increased by 5 feet and your jump distance is tripled.

entries.name: Step Propulsor
entries: Once per long rest, you may activate your step propulsors. For the next minute, your walking speed is doubled.

Cyborgs replace flesh with artifice in pursuit of strength, precision, and transcendence. Many become adventurers because danger, discovery, and treasure offer the fastest path to further augmentation.

entries.name: Society
entries: Cyborgs rarely form societies of their own. Instead, they emerge from other cultures and either remain within them as altered outliers or leave to pursue ever greater self-improvement.

entries.name: Names
entries: Cyborgs often keep the names of the people they once were, though some adopt sharper, more mechanical monikers after extensive modification.

race.name: House Dragon
entries: Small, quadrupedal dragons who aren’t afraid to go out adventuring with their bipedal peers.

entries.name: Age
entries: House dragons reach maturity in about 10 years, and live to be about 50; not nearly as long as larger dragons, mainly due to the breeding process.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Alignment tendencies were bred out of your species long ago, so your alignment tends to lean toward whatever group of non‑House Dragons you grew up with.

entries.name: Size
entries: House dragons stand 2 to 3 feet at the shoulder and weigh around 100 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Feral Stance
entries: You are a quadruped. Your forepaws have opposable thumbs, allowing you to manipulate objects, but you cannot properly wield weapons made for bipeds. You cannot use two‑handed weapons or dual‑wield, and you cannot don armor made for bipeds (you require barding). Shields do not grant AC bonuses. Actions requiring free hands (loading weapons, drinking potions, casting spells with material components) cost 5 feet of movement.

entries.name: Natural Defenses
entries: Your scales provide protection. You gain a +1 bonus to AC.

entries.name: Natural Offenses
entries: You have a claw attack and a bite attack, considered unarmed strikes. They deal 1d6 + Strength modifier damage. Claws deal slashing damage and have the finesse property; bites deal piercing damage.

entries.name: Big Eater
entries: You have advantage on checks to resist poison and disease transmitted via food and drink, and on checks to consume large or questionable amounts of food and drink.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

House dragons are miniature, wingless dragons bred to live indoors. They lack breath weapons and wings, but retain draconic charisma and resilience. They serve as companions, familiars, and even adventurers.

entries.name: Society
entries: House dragons live among other species rather than forming their own societies. They are social and adapt to the culture they are raised in.

entries.name: Names
entries: House dragons adopt the naming conventions of the cultures they live with, rather than having their own.

entries.name: Random Height and Weight
entries: Base Height: 2'0" + 1d12 in.
Base Weight: 100 lb. × (1d12)

variants.name: Coward Mode
entries: Variant rules for players who don’t want to deal with quadruped restrictions:
- You may apply proficiency to a single one‑handed weapon wielded in your mouth.
- You may fulfill somatic components by wagging your tail, and material components by merely having them on your person.
- Movement penalties from Feral Stance no longer apply.

race.name: Kemonomimi
entries: Kemonomimi are humanoids with animal-like features such as ears, tails, wings, or horns. Their appearance varies widely depending on their subrace.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

subraces.name: Bear-Eared
entries: You have the small round bear ears and a stubby bear tail.

entries.name: Hibernation Preparation
entries: Bears need to hibernate through the winter, but you don't. After eating at least 1 pound of food beyond your requirement for the day, you gain 1d6 temporary hit points for the next hour.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Bears have a good sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Mama Bear
entries: When an ally creature is hit while you're within 5 feet of them, you can use your reaction to do a melee weapon attack on the attacker if they are within your weapon's reach. You regain usage of this feature when you finish a short or long rest.

subraces.name: Cow-Eared
entries: You have floppy cow ears, a long furry cow tail and the horns of a cow/bull.

entries.name: Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line towards a creature and then make a successful attack with your horns, the target must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. The DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Horns
entries: In addition to your cow-ears, you have natural horns. When you do an unarmed attack, you can instead use your horns to do 1d6 piercing damage using your Strength as attack and damage modifiers as normal.

entries.name: Improved Digestion
entries: Your single stomach has more digestive compartments, and thus you can eat things humans normally can't digest like grass and it'd count as food for you. However, you need twice the amount of food a normal Medium-sized creature requires each day.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

subraces.name: Wolf-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a wolf and fluffy wolf's tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Smell and Hearing
entries: Wolves have a good sense of smell and hearing, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pack Tactics
entries: You have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the your allies is within 5 ft. of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

subraces.name: Lion-Eared
entries: You have the Fluffy ears of a Lion and fluffy Lion's tail.

entries.name: Teeth and Claws
entries: You have retractable claws and very sharp teeth, this allows you to do an unarmed attack of 1D8 + Str with your Bite or 1D6 + Str with your claws.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Lions have a good sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pack Tactics
entries: You have advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the your allies is within 5 ft. of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

subraces.name: Cat-Eared
entries: You have the triangle-shaped ears of a cat and a long furry cat tail.

entries.name: Cat Burglar
entries: As a cat, you enter and exit without being noticed. You are proficient in Stealth.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Hunter
entries: You are a natural hunter, and you focus on your prey with conviction. You can cast the hunter's mark spell once. You regain usage of this feature after you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Naturally Graceful
entries: Cats always land on their feet. You have resistance to fall damage.

subraces.name: Feather-Eared
entries: You have small feathered wings where your ears are, a feathered bird tail, along with their feathered wings.

entries.name: Bird Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have bird wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground. You may not fly in normal backpacks or armor. The cost to alter your gear to be suitable for flight is equal to half the cost of the gear.

entries.name: Keen Sight
entries: Birds are very good at spotting and finding prey. You are proficient in Perception.

subraces.name: Raccoon-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a raccoon and a stripped furry fail

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Natural Bandit
entries: As a scavenger, raccoons naturally steal things. You are proficient in Sleight of Hand and Thieves' Tools.

entries.name: Tree Climber
entries: Raccoons can climb trees, but since you have human-like hands, you can't climb as well. When attempting to climb trees with an Athletics check, you may use Dexterity as the modifier instead of Strength.

subraces.name: Horse-Eared
entries: You have the ears of a horse or pony, along with their long-haired tail.

entries.name: Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then attempt to shove it to knock it prone, the target instead must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. The DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Swift Steed
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Work Horse
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can’t use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Pig-Eared
entries: You have the large ears of a pig and their coiled tails

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Pigs are very good at foraging with their nose, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Pig Fat
entries: Your fat gives you more endurance, your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

subraces.name: Sheep-Eared
entries: You have the long pointed ears of a sheep, along with their wool-like hair, curved horns, and cute little tail.

entries.name: Horns
entries: In addition to your sheep-ears, you have natural curved horns. Your unarmed strikes using your Horns deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Sure-Footed
entries: You have advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock you prone.

entries.name: Woolly Hair
entries: Instead of hair, you grow wool on your body instead, giving you extra protection. When unarmored, your Armor Class is equal to 12 + your Dexterity modifier.

subraces.name: Bat-Eared
entries: You have the large wide ears of a bat, bat wings growing out your back, and possibly a tail if your species had one.

entries.name: Bat Roost
entries: When grabbing fixtures on a ceiling (such as chandeliers or stalactites), or long appendages sticking out (such as a flagpole sticking on the side or branches of trees), you can hang upside-down on them without making an ability check if you occupy both your hands on it. You cannot hang upside down on smooth surfaces where there's nothing to grab onto. However, you're not a real bat and you have human hands instead of claws, so the blood will rush to your head and you will lose your grip. Thus, you can only hang like this for a minute without moving. At the end of the minute, if you did not move, you start falling.

entries.name: Bat Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have bat wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. But due to the short wingspan due to being a kemonomimi, while flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any medium or heavy armor, you may not fly.

entries.name: Echolocation
entries: You can perceive your surroundings without relying on sight within a specific radius. You have a blindsight range of 10 feet. You can't use your blindsight while deafened.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Chromatic)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Chromatic Protection
entries: Starting at 3rd level, as an action, you can channel your draconic energies to protect yourself. For 10 minutes, you become resistant to the damage type associated with your Chromatic Ancestry. Once you use this trait, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Metallic)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Metallic Breath
