entries: At 5th level, you gain a second breath weapon. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhale in a 15-foot cone. The saving throw DC for this breath is 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. Whenever you use this feature, choose one: Unnerving Breath. Each creature in the cone must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the start of your next turn. Repulsion Breath.* Each creature in the cone must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 20 feet away from you and fall to the ground.Once you use your Metal Breath, you can't do it again until you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Dragon-Eared (Gem)
entries: You have the wings and horns of a dragon along with their scaled tails.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy, just like a dragon. Choose one type of dragon on the Dragonborn's Draconic Ancestry table determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. This counts as your draconic ancestry, but it does not give you the damage resistance. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one.The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Wings
entries: In addition to your ears and tail, you have dragon wings between the shoulder blade and hip area and you gain a flight speed of 30 feet. If you are wearing armor you are not proficient in or any Medium or Heavy armor, you may not fly. Must keep moving. While flying, if you do not move at least half your speed during your turn, you fall to the ground.

entries.name: Gem Rarity
entries: Unlike many Dragonborns, you gain a Draconic breath that is different from the others. Choose one from Psychic, Radiant, Strength, Necrotic, or Thunder.

entries.name: Psionic Mind
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You don't have to share a language with the creature, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. You have the creature talk to you for up to 1 hour. To do this, she needs to see and be aware to do this or you will terminate this effect (no action needed).

entries.name: Gift of the Gem Dragons
entries: You know the Silver Mind trick, which you use at will. Starting at 3rd level, you gain Tasha's steric laughter spell. Starting at 5th level, you gain the Detect Thoughts spell. Once you use one of the high-level spells, you can't use it again after a short or long rest. Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma is your ability to cast these spells.

subraces.name: Mouse-Eared
entries: You have the large round ears of a mouse and their long pointed tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Smell
entries: Mice have an amazing sense of smell, thus you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Nimble
entries: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

entries.name: Scurry
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

subraces.name: Monkey-Eared
entries: You look almost entirely human, except for your long furry monkey tail.

entries.name: Prehensile Tail
entries: Your monkey tail can be used to hold and manipulate objects. However, your tail is not as strong as your arms, and thus, you cannot use your tail to hold things heavier than 10 pounds, nor use it to wield weapons with the heavy or two-handed property.

entries.name: Smart Monkey
entries: You can speak, read and write one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Tree Climber
entries: Monkeys climb and swing from trees, you are proficient in Acrobatics.

subraces.name: Rabbit-Eared
entries: You have a pair of floppy or pointed rabbit ears in addition to a small fluff ball tail.

entries.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 40 feet.

entries.name: Lucky Rabbit's Foot
entries: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

entries.name: Superior Hearing
entries: Your large rabbit ears grants you an increased awareness of your surroundings compared to other kemonomimi. You are proficient in Perception.

subraces.name: Squirrel-Eared
entries: You have pointed squirrel ears in addition to your large fluffy curved tail.

entries.name: Constant Prey
entries: People are always out for your big fluffy tail, you have a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) score.

entries.name: Forager
entries: Squirrels can naturally find food. You are proficient in Survival and you have advantage on Survival checks when foraging.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: Squirrels are very good at noticing threats, you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

subraces.name: Dog-Eared
entries: You have a variety of dog ears to pick from in addition to their wide variety of different tails, appearing like the breed you are.

entries.name: Alpha Dog
entries: You are inspiring and a natural leader. You know the guidance cantrip. Additionally, you can cast heroism using Charisma as your spellcasting ability modifier. You regain the use of this feature when you complete a long rest.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: Dogs have good hearing and a sense of smell, you have proficiency in the Perception skill.

entries.name: Puppy Dog Eyes
entries: No one can withstand your puppy dog eyes. You gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

subraces.name: Fin-Eared
entries: You have the pectoral fins of the fish you are where your ears are, but no tail, however your seemingly human pair of legs transform and appear as your tail fin allowing you to move very fast in the water.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: It is very dark underwater where you live. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fins
entries: Your legs are your tail. As a bonus action, you can combine your legs for form your fish tail. Anything you're wearing from the waist down is automatically removed and is dropped on the floor unless it can fit your legs' new form. While in this form, your base walking speed is reduced to 15 feet and you gain a base swim speed of 50 feet.

entries.name: Gills
entries: You are the kemonomimi of the sea, and thus you can breathe air and water. You are immune to drowning.

subraces.name: Fox-Eared
entries: You have the pointed ears of a fox and their bushy tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fox Cunning
entries: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

entries.name: Foxy
entries: Your foxy demeanor makes you hard to resist, before making a Charisma based skill check you can choose to grant yourself advantage. You regain usage of this feature when you complete a long rest.

subraces.name: Tiger-Eared
entries: You have small ears and a large tiger tail.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Born Survivor
entries: Choose 2 skills from: Athletics, Perception, Survival, Insight, Investigation and Nature to become proficient in.

entries.name: Sharp nails
entries: Your nails are like a usable natural weapon. You deal 1d6 slashing damage instead of bludgeoning.

entries.name: Ferocious Roar
entries: You enter a state of rage that reveals itself in a scream of animalistic fury. All creatures within 60 feet of you must make a Wisdom Saving Throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + Your Strength score) ​​or become frightened of you for 1 minute. You can only do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses after a long rest.

race.name: Kumiho
entries: You are a 9 tailed foxes that like to take the form of humanoids, you gain the following traits:

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Charisma score increases by 2.

entries.name: Age
entries: Kumiho reach adulthood around 150 years and can live up to a millenia.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most Kumiho tend to be chaotic, but on occasion they can be lawful to those they are fond of. Kumiho have been known to be both good or evil.

entries.name: Size
entries: 4 and half to 5 and a half feet tall in human or hybrid form and the size of a large dog in fox form. Your size is Medium in human and hybrid form and Small in fox form.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet. your movement is increased to 40 feet in fox form.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Incarnation
entries: You are able to change form to that of a human with your ears and tail(s) still visible or to a human-fox hybrid form. While in normal fox form, people cannot see you as anything more than a normal fox, unless they succeed a Perception check with a DC of 10 + your level. Switching forms requires an action. When in fox form, the only spells you can cast are cantrips.

entries.name: Tooth and Claw
entries: Kumiho have natural weapons, their claws do 1d4 + your Strength or your Dexterity modifier. When in fox form, you also have a bite attack that does 1d6 + your Strength or your Dexterity modifier.

entries.name: Kumiho Tails
entries: You gain more tails as you gain more power starting with a second tail at level 4 you gain 1 tail every 2 levels up to 9 tails at level 18th.

entries.name: Acute Senses
entries: Kumiho have extraordinary senses. You have advantage on Perception checks.

entries.name: Sneaky
entries: You are proficient with the Stealth skill, and have advantage on Stealth checks but only while in fox form.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Fey, Descendent, Half-breed, Draconic.

entries.name: Physical Description
entries: At first glance, a kumiho is identical to a fox in every way, with the exception of long slender legs and angled bodies that rival the size of a large dog and most have 9 tails by the time they reach full adulthood. Their fur can come in different colors, but red with white tipped ears and tail is the most common coloration to be found aside from black tipped tail and ears or pure white fur. Their eyes illuminate with vibrant colors, making their iris have a faint glow in darkness.

Many kumiho like to travel in a humanoid form that displays their claws, ears, and tail(s). When a kumiho takes the form of a humanoid, they take the appearance of how their subconscious believes they would look as a human, but they always take the same appearance. They can also take a hybrid form, blending the appearance of their human and fox forms into an anthropomorphic fox.

Kumiho look very young for their actual age. One that is 100 years of age might look like a teenager. Around 150 years, their appearance halts around a human’s mid 20's until they reach 800 years. In the course of those final two centuries, they begin to age into late adulthood and finally their senior years prior to dying of old age.

entries.name: History
entries: Kumiho have roots among the fey as a race of mystical foxes incarnated from the souls of children who die at a young age. They often remain in the feywilds of their birth but the rare kumiho will venture out to see the worlds, hiding their true nature from curious eyes as they use fey magic to shift into a human form.

entries.name: Society
entries: Kumiho are widely believed to be a myth or thought to be the ancestors of Kitsune. Many scholars believe that Kumiho blood has powerful magic properties worth exploiting. When discovered, Kumiho will often claim they are a typical kitsune with a genetic mutation to have multiple tails. As Kumiho are often attractive and easy on the eyes, they are typically well liked in social interactions, except their hybrid form is usually unsettling to others.

entries.name: Male
entries: Mako, Kobe, Kurt, Kurama

entries.name: Female
entries: Holo, Myra, Lira, Asuna

race.name: Lopunny
entries: Graceful, beautiful, and rather short, these naturally timid bipedal lagomorphs resemble rabbits with long ears and fluffy fur.

entries.name: Age
entries: Bunearies reach maturity and develop into Lopunnies at 2 years and can live for approximately 12 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Lopunnies usually lean toward neutral or lawful alignments, though mostly neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: The average height for a Lopunny is barely 4 feet tall and usually weighs a little over 70 lbs. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Timid Nature
entries: You have disadvantage on saving throws against being frightened.

entries.name: Burst of Speed
entries: When you take the Dash action, you can move an additional 10 feet.

entries.name: Leg Up
entries: You have proficiency with using your legs to perform unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

entries.name: Cute Charm
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Limber
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being paralyzed.

entries.name: Heightened Hearing
entries: Whenever you make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check that involves hearing, you are considered proficient in the {@skill Perception} skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and your choice of Elvish or Sylvan.
Graceful and cautious, Lopunnies are timid lagomorphs who prefer to flee danger but can deliver powerful kicks when forced to fight.

entries.name: History
entries: Lopunnies, along with their Buneary young, originated from the forests of the Sinnoh region, though they can be found almost anywhere. They are favorites among coordinators and contest participants due to their charm.

entries.name: Society
entries: Lopunnies usually live in colonies, caring for their young. They shed their fur twice a year, which is often collected and used in clothing.

entries.name: Names
entries: Despite appearing feminine, Lopunnies can be either male or female. Their names are usually Common or Elvish.

entries.name: Random Height and Weight
entries: Base Height: 3'6" + 2d4 in.
Base Weight: 60 lb. × (1d4)

race.name: Pipe Fox
entries: Curious fox spirits with serpentine bodies, pipe foxes are subtle collectors of knowledge who trade secrets as readily as coin.

entries.name: Age
entries: Pipe foxes mature by age 8 and can live for around 120 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Pipe foxes are usually neutral, balancing playful curiosity with careful self-preservation.

entries.name: Size
entries: Pipe foxes are small, lithe creatures with long bodies and foxlike heads. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a climbing speed of 20 feet.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Lorekeeper
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill.

entries.name: Natural Bite
entries: You have a bite attack that you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, the strike deals 1d4 piercing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Fox Magic
entries: You know the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast {@spell invisibility} with this trait once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

A pipe fox is a furry, serpentine fox spirit that values knowledge above nearly everything else. Pipe foxes hoard secrets, lore, and rumors, sharing them only when they are offered something equally valuable in return.

entries.name: Society
entries: Pipe foxes rarely gather in large communities. Most instead attach themselves to scholars, mystics, or travelers whose lives promise a steady flow of stories and hidden truths.

entries.name: Names
entries: Pipe fox names tend to be short, musical, and easy to whisper: Kiko, Nari, Suzu, Tama, Yori, and Zeni are common examples.

race.name: Robot
entries: Artificial beings of metal and circuitry, robots are manufactured constructs that have gained sentience.

entries.name: Age
entries: Robots do not age naturally. They can function indefinitely with proper maintenance, though most break down after a few centuries.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Robots tend toward lawful alignments, following their programming, though free‑willed robots may adopt any alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Robots are built in humanoid form, typically between 5 and 7 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Constructed Nature
entries: You are a mass of metals, wires, and whatnot. Your creature type is construct instead of humanoid. As such, spells like cure wounds don't affect you, and you are immune to spells like crown of madness or dominate person because they specifically target humanoids. You are immune to poison damage, being poisoned, and diseases. You do not need to eat or breathe, and you cannot attempt to do so. Rather than sleeping, you enter an inactive state for 6 hours each day. You do not dream in this state; you are fully aware of your surroundings and notice approaching enemies and other events as normal.

entries.name: Integrated Protection
entries: Your AC can never be lower than 10 + your Constitution modifier.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common.

entries.name: War Proficiency
entries: You are proficient with any four martial weapons of your choice.

entries.name: Fast
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Battle Dash
entries: You can move up to half your walking speed after taking an attack action. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses when you finish a long rest.

subraces.name: Manufacturer Robot
languages: one other language of your choice

entries.name: Mercantile Programming
entries: You learn one language of your choice.

entries.name: Manufacture
entries: You are proficient in an artisan's tool of your choice. Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a single simple item worth no more than 10 gp. The item should be relevant to the nature of the tool you are proficient in. For example, leatherworker's tools would allow you to make a simple belt or a cloak. Glassblower's tools might allow you to make a simple, but crude vase.

entries.name: Quality Check
entries: Whenever you make an ({@skill Investigation}) check related to defects in a product, you are considered proficient in the Investigation skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Medical Sensor
entries: As an action, you can sense if any creatures within 60 feet of you are currently affected by poisons or diseases. You do not know what the poison or disease is.

entries.name: First Aid
entries: You can cast cure wounds a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, using Wisdom as your spellcasting modifier. You regain use of this trait after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Medical Knowledge
entries: You are proficient in Medicine.
Robots are manufactured constructs, often created for war, labor, or specialized tasks. Over time, some have developed free will and independence, becoming adventurers or companions.

entries.name: Society
entries: Robots do not form societies of their own, instead integrating into the cultures of their creators or wandering as individuals.

entries.name: Names
entries: Robots are often designated by serial numbers or functional titles, though some adopt personal names.

subrace.name: Fey
entries: Your connection to the earliest of kumiho is strong, and your fey-like presence reflects this.

entries.name: Fey Magic
entries: You know the produce flame cantrip, with the exception that your flame is blue. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the charm person spell once and need to finish a long rest before you can cast it again. When you reach 5th level, you can also cast the phantasmal force spell once and need to finish a long rest before you can cast it again. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Sylvan Aura
entries: You give off an aura of fey magic constantly. Creatures that can see or detect magic see a miasma of magic surrounding you in a 5 foot radius. This aura alerts you to unseen threats within 5 feet. This acts as blindsense up to 5 feet, only telling you a direction, not position or identity of the creature or object.

subrace.name: Descendent
entries: Your parents have strayed from the feywild forests and have adapted to surviving with their fox form.

entries.name: Wild Survivor
entries: You were raised in the wild and know how to survive in the woods. You have advantage on Nature and Survival checks.

entries.name: Natural Hunter
entries: Your ability to hunt has taught you how to stalk and pounce. You have advantage with Stealth checks in any form. Also, you may use your Dexterity score when determining your jumping distance and add your Dexterity modifier to any Athletics checks associated with jumping.

subrace.name: Half-breed
entries: With such beautiful human forms, it was only a matter of time for a half-breed kumiho to be born.

entries.name: Half Blood
entries: In your human form your claws, tail, and ears are not visible if you wish to hide them. Creatures can identify you as a half-blood kumiho using an Investigation check with a DC of 12 + your level. Your "human" form takes the appearance of your half-blood parentage instead of human but you are still Medium sized.

entries.name: Family Connection
entries: Select a race other than kumiho to be the base of your other parent. You may select a subrace from that race and benefit from it's traits. If the chosen race does't have a subrace (e.g. Human) you may choose one trait from the race. The chosen trait can't be ability score increase, alignment, speed, size, or age.

subrace.name: Draconic
entries: Somewhere in your lineage, dragon blood or a dragon’s blessing found its way to you. Unlike other kumiho, you have a pair of dragon-like horns and eyes, and some parts of your dragon body may even have patches of scales. The horns and eyes are visible in your human form. Pick a dragon color from the table below during character creation to determine your subrace trait elements. **Discuss this subrace with you DM before selecting it.

entries.name: Ancestral Breath
entries: Your bloodline has blessed you with a breath attack. As a action, you may use a breath attack matching the element and shape of the dragon color you have selected from the table below. The damage increases to 2d6 at 6th level, 3d6 at 11th level, and 4d6 at 16th level. The target creature may make the appropriate save with a DC of 12 + Constitution modifier. On a successful save they take half damage. You may use this trait once and regain its use after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Form
entries: You can take the form of a dragon once. This form is a human sized chromatic or metallic dragon of the color you selected. You are medium while in this form, have a fly speed of 30ft, and have advantage to Constitution saves. You can freely use your ancestral breath while in this form a number of times equal to your level, but lose your ancestral breath. This transformation lasts 1 minute and you regain its use after finishing a long rest.

entries.name: Dragon Type
entries: You can select one of the following Dragon Types to be your dragon form.
